design_philosophy.core_loop: Enter room → collect hearts scattered throughout maze → open treasure chest with gem → exit to next room. Navigate obstacles, avoid/trap enemies, push blocks, use limited shots to egg enemies temporarily.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Spatial puzzle-solving under threat of roaming enemies with varying movement and attack patterns. Time pressure from enemy AI and limited shot resources creates moment-to-moment decision-making.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Rescue Princess Lala by solving increasingly complex logical puzzles and navigating dangerous castle rooms as the round hero Lolo.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rejects action-dominant platformer conventions of its era in favor of logic-first puzzle design. Uses cute character design (round anthropomorphic protagonist) to contrast with demanding spatial reasoning. Nintendo's stated goal: 'stretching the kids' imaginations' rather than reflexes. Critics noted it was 'not yet another cutesy platformer' and represented a 'challenge of logic, not brute force.'

difficulty.lives_system: One life lost when shot by or touched by certain enemies. Player can restart any screen at cost of one life.
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-room restart available at life cost. 50 rooms across 10 floors of 5 rooms each.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player can restart current screen indefinitely (burning lives), indicating puzzle-first design philosophy. Enemy patterns are fixed/predictable rather than adaptive, allowing spatial memorization and planning.

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary power-ups on specific screens: ability to smash rocks, build bridges. Varied items in heart frames can trap or defeat specific enemy types.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Collect required hearts to open treasure chest and retrieve gem, which opens exit to next room/floor. Hierarchical progression: 5 rooms per floor × 10 floors.
replayability_hooks: Password system enabling non-linear room access (evidenced by password generator tools in modding community)
50 distinct puzzle rooms with different enemy configurations and spatial solutions
Multiple enemy types (Skull, Medusa, Rocky, snake-like creatures) requiring adapted strategies per room
Cult following suggests high replay value; critics noted it 'will last for hours' despite completable in one day

world_building_techniques: Opening cutscene showing Lolo walking toward castle (evidenced by unused sideways walking sprites for inter-area cutscenes)
Narrative frame: rescue Princess Lala from evil King Egger's castle
Environmental storytelling through room/floor progression implying castle exploration and ascension toward final confrontation
Simple premise allowing focus on puzzle mechanics rather than narrative complexity

danger_signals: Evil King Egger as antagonist figure
Princess Lala kidnapped/imprisoned, motivating player progression
Castle setting as dungeon/hostile domain requiring traversal
environmental_storytelling: Castle rooms progressively increase in complexity, implying deeper penetration into enemy territory
Enemy variety (Skull, Medusa, Rocky) suggests mythological/dark aesthetic despite cute protagonist
Treasure chests and gems as magical reward objects signaling fantasy setting
Room layouts and enemy placement create implicit narrative: player must outwit and trap adversaries rather than defeat them outright
